hypothesis: A cohesive optional rendering and technical execution package can provide operator-grade browser, robots, sitemap, hreflang, redirect, indexability, Core Web Vitals, preload, and structured-data evidence without duplicating URL safety, PageSpeed, evidence, or command authorities.

strongest_objections: The browser dependency must remain optional; forcing browser binaries into core would worsen installation and maintenance burden.
Fixture rendering and injected browser responses do not prove a live Chromium network run, so browser live parity must remain GAP_OPEN.
Technical indexability must not be presented as proof that Google indexed, will index, or selected the page's declared canonical.
Hreflang inspection on one page cannot prove reciprocal return links or inspect header and XML implementations.
Schema syntax and baseline checks cannot be represented as Google rich-result eligibility.
residual_risks: A real Chromium download and controlled-site smoke test remain blocked by DNS resolution in the execution environment.
Live public-web behavior can differ behind enterprise proxies, WAFs, consent layers, and bot controls.

strongest_objections: Every navigation and redirect target, including browser subresources, must use the canonical public-host policy to prevent private-network access.
The implementation must reuse the existing PageSpeed adapter, command registry, ToolDispatcher, and AdapterResult rather than create parallel authorities.
Browser and HTTP work must have hard timeout, response, redirect, screenshot, and collection ceilings.
Playwright must be independently installable and removable, with missing binaries returning NOT_CONFIGURED rather than a stack trace.
Structured-data generation must remain allowlisted and operator-fact-only to avoid fabricated business, offer, review, or medical claims.
residual_risks: Playwright browser releases remain coupled to Playwright package versions and require operational refresh after upgrades.
DNS-rebinding and proxy policy remain deployment-environment concerns despite per-request public-host checks.

lessons: Browser capability belongs in an optional independently removable pack, while health and fixture behavior belong in core tests.
Every redirect and browser request must be revalidated rather than trusting the initial public URL.
Technical eligibility, Google index state, and Google-selected canonical are separate evidence products.
Structured-data detection, baseline syntax validation, and rich-result eligibility must remain distinct claims.
A blocked browser download smoke test must remain visible and cannot be converted into fixture-proven live parity.
next_action: Submit the green code contract for normal squash merge while preserving browser and controlled-site live-smoke parity as GAP_OPEN; rerun the smoke test in an environment with Playwright CDN access.
